title: The WSL distribution is not cleaned up after `podman machine init` failure
body:
### Issue Description

After a failure of the command `podman machine init` on WSL:
- `podman machine ls` doesn't show the failed-to-create-machine (and that's fine)
- `wsl --list` shows the failed-to-create-machine (and the subsequent `podman machine init` will fail).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Download the wrong arch podman installer (i.e., [amd64](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.6.1/podman-installer-windows-amd64.exe) if running on arm64 Windows) and run it
2. Run `podman machine init`: this fails as expected
3. Uninstall Podman and reinstall using the right arch installer (i.e., [arm64](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.6.1/podman-installer-windows-arm64.exe) if running on arm64 Windows)
4. Run `podman machine init`: this fails with the error `vm "podman-machine-default" already exists on hypervisor`, but `podman machine ls` doesn't show the machine, and the commands `podman machine rm` and `podman machine reset` fail to remove the WSL instance

### Describe the results you received

After a `podman machine init` failure, there is an inconsistency between what `podman` and `wsl` see:

```pwsh
> podman machien ls
NAME        VM TYPE     CREATED     LAST UP     CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
> wsl --list
podman-machine-default
```

### Describe the results you expected

When `podman machine init` fails, it should clean up the WSL distribution before exiting.

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: windows/arm64
provider: wsl
version: 5.6.1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Output of the failing `podman machine init` command:

```pwsh
> podman machine init
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
